pages.desc: A sak-yant tattoo stencil captioned in modern Thai ยันต์ดอกบัวทอง (yant dok bua thong, the golden-lotus yant). A single tall vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script runs the length of the leaf like
pages.descFull: A sak-yant tattoo stencil captioned in modern Thai ยันต์ดอกบัวทอง (yant dok bua thong, the golden-lotus yant). A single tall vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script runs the length of the leaf like a spine tattoo, rising to a full-blown lotus blossom at the crown; above it three flame-spike unalom sceptres stand like antennae. The lotus marks purity and blossoming fortune; the script column is the katha inked down the back. A design meant to be traced and needled along the spine.

pages.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์สาริกาคู่ (yant sarika khu, the paired-sarika yant). Two facing sarika (myna) birds perch head-to-head atop a long vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script, three u
pages.descFull: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์สาริกาคู่ (yant sarika khu, the paired-sarika yant). Two facing sarika (myna) birds perch head-to-head atop a long vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script, three unalom spikes rising above them. The sarika is the bird of sweet, persuasive speech: the paired form is a metta-mahaniyom charm for charm, eloquence and drawing affection — inked as a spine or nape column.

pages.desc: Two sak-yant stencils side by side. Left, captioned ยันต์ ๕ แถว (yant ha thaew, the five-row yant): five parallel vertical lines of dense Tham Lanna script, each capped by an unalom spike — the five-l
pages.descFull: Two sak-yant stencils side by side. Left, captioned ยันต์ ๕ แถว (yant ha thaew, the five-row yant): five parallel vertical lines of dense Tham Lanna script, each capped by an unalom spike — the five-line kong-krapan protection grid. Right, captioned as a lattice yant: a diagonal crosshatch net filled with Tham Lanna numerals under five unalom, a woven number-mesh (turtle/net pattern) worked for enclosing protection. Both are column tattoos meant to be traced onto skin.

pages.desc: A sak-yant sheet of two money-bag roundels, captioned ยันต์ถุงเงินถุงทอง … โภคทรัพย์ (yant thung ngoen thung thong, the silver-bag gold-bag yant; phokha sap, wealth). Each is a drawn pouch: a quartere
pages.descFull: A sak-yant sheet of two money-bag roundels, captioned ยันต์ถุงเงินถุงทอง … โภคทรัพย์ (yant thung ngoen thung thong, the silver-bag gold-bag yant; phokha sap, wealth). Each is a drawn pouch: a quartered Na-square of four seed-syllables set inside a round bag, tied at the neck, ringed by a circle of Tham Lanna script and crowned with an unalom finial. A prosperity charm to draw and hold money — inked for wealth and abundance.

pages.desc: Sak-yant tracing stencil. Two "yant dok bua" (lotus yantras) drawn one above the other in fine black outline; caption below reads ๑ ยันต์ดอกบัว ฯ ("No. 1, Lotus Yantra"). Each yant is a stylised lotus
pages.descFull:
Sak-yant tracing stencil. Two "yant dok bua" (lotus yantras) drawn one above the other in fine black outline; caption below reads ๑ ยันต์ดอกบัว ฯ ("No. 1, Lotus Yantra"). Each yant is a stylised lotus blossom crowned by a flame-shaped unalome finial (tapering spire with a curled tip) flanked by small Khom finial ticks. A single Khom (ขอม) sacred-script syllable sits at the heart of each lotus; a curved arc of further Khom syllables runs beneath the petals. No colour or shading — a line stencil meant to be copied onto skin or cloth.
Tradition — the lotus yant is a Buddhist purity emblem (rising unstained above defilement); commonly given for metta (loving-kindness) and clean, rising fortune.

pages.desc: Sak-yant tracing stencil. A single composite yant: a central lotus with a flame/unalome finial and three spire-markers above (the middle one a rounded uposatha/spade marker), flanked left and right by
pages.descFull:
Sak-yant tracing stencil. A single composite yant: a central lotus with a flame/unalome finial and three spire-markers above (the middle one a rounded uposatha/spade marker), flanked left and right by a mirrored pair of winged birds (sarika/hamsa) whose plumes curl outward. A wide arc of Khom (ขอม) sacred-script syllables sweeps beneath the whole design. Caption reads ๑ ยันต์สาริกาคู่ ฯ ("No. 1, Paired-Sarika Yantra"). Fine black outline only, a stencil for tracing.
Tradition — the sarika bird governs sweet, persuasive speech; the paired-sarika yant is a classic metta-mahaniyom charm for charm, likeability and winning words.

pages.desc: Sak-yant tracing stencil, a second "yant sarika khu" (paired-sarika) variant. A central lotus topped by a flame/unalome finial with three spire-markers rises between two facing sarika birds; a central
pages.descFull:
Sak-yant tracing stencil, a second "yant sarika khu" (paired-sarika) variant. A central lotus topped by a flame/unalome finial with three spire-markers rises between two facing sarika birds; a central vertical Khom column drops from the lotus, and a dense arc of Khom (ขอม) syllables curves along the base. Caption reads ๑ ยันต์สาริกาคู่ ฯ. Black outline stencil for tracing onto skin or cloth.
Tradition — same metta-mahaniyom (sweet-speech, likeability) function as the other paired-sarika yant; the two birds face each other to signify harmonious, winning speech between people.

pages.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์นกยูง (yant nok yung, the peacock yant). Two peacocks with long sweeping tail-plumes stand facing, flanking a central tied money-bag/heart, the whole enclosed in a br
pages.descFull: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์นกยูง (yant nok yung, the peacock yant). Two peacocks with long sweeping tail-plumes stand facing, flanking a central tied money-bag/heart, the whole enclosed in a broad oval arc of Tham Lanna spell-script with a small unalom crown. The peacock is a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty, allure and radiant charm — a charm to be seen and favoured, traced across the back or chest.

pages.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์หงส์คำ (yant hong kham, the golden-hamsa yant). A single hamsa (celestial swan/goose) rises with wings spread and a long curling scrolled tail, wrapped in an arc of T
pages.descFull: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์หงส์คำ (yant hong kham, the golden-hamsa yant). A single hamsa (celestial swan/goose) rises with wings spread and a long curling scrolled tail, wrapped in an arc of Tham Lanna script with three unalom spikes above. The hong is a bearer of grace, high status and auspicious ascent — a metta-and-fortune charm inked as a rising figure on shoulder or back.

pages.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์พญาไก่ฟ้า (yant phaya kai fa, the lord-pheasant / firebird yant). A crested pheasant-phoenix sweeps across the leaf with a long trailing plumed tail, a small Na-circl
pages.descFull: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์พญาไก่ฟ้า (yant phaya kai fa, the lord-pheasant / firebird yant). A crested pheasant-phoenix sweeps across the leaf with a long trailing plumed tail, a small Na-circle and unalom at its crown, the body ringed and underscored by Tham Lanna spell-script. The kai fa is a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty, allure and charm — a stencil for attraction and favour, traced across the back or shoulder.

pages.desc: A sak-yant sheet of two butterfly stencils, captioned ยันต์ผีเสื้อ (yant phi suea, the butterfly yant). Above, a symmetrical open-winged butterfly with patterned wings, an unalom crown and an arc of T
pages.descFull: A sak-yant sheet of two butterfly stencils, captioned ยันต์ผีเสื้อ (yant phi suea, the butterfly yant). Above, a symmetrical open-winged butterfly with patterned wings, an unalom crown and an arc of Tham Lanna script; below, a single butterfly banked into a crescent of script. The butterfly is a metta-mahaniyom charm of beauty and attraction — worked to make the wearer alluring and beloved, inked small on nape or shoulder.

pages.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned as a Hanuman yant. Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaps in three-quarter view, brandishing a staff/trident and a pennoned flag, mouth open, tail curling; his limbs and t
pages.descFull: A sak-yant stencil captioned as a Hanuman yant. Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaps in three-quarter view, brandishing a staff/trident and a pennoned flag, mouth open, tail curling; his limbs and torso are covered in Na seed-syllables and the figure is wrapped in a sweeping arc of Tham Lanna script. This is a kong-krapan / invulnerability and power charm — Hanuman lends strength, boldness and immunity to harm, a favoured back or chest tattoo.

pages.desc: A sak-yant sheet of two ruesi (lersi) heads, captioned ยันต์เศียรฤๅษี พ่อแก่ (yant sian ruesi, pho kae — the head of the hermit-teacher, the Old Father). Above, a frontal face of the ascetic master cr
pages.descFull: A sak-yant sheet of two ruesi (lersi) heads, captioned ยันต์เศียรฤๅษี พ่อแก่ (yant sian ruesi, pho kae — the head of the hermit-teacher, the Old Father). Above, a frontal face of the ascetic master crowned with a tiered headdress under three unalom spikes and an arc of script; below, a profile head of a bearded ruesi in a peaked tiger-skin cap. Pho Kae is the primordial teacher honoured in the wai khru — this stencil places the lineage-master himself on the skin as protector and source of the craft.

pages.desc: A finely inked lotus blossom standing alone at the centre of an otherwise blank leaf: a full flower drawn in fine hatching, layered pointed petals opening around a beaded seed-pod crown, with a small
pages.descFull: A finely inked lotus blossom standing alone at the centre of an otherwise blank leaf: a full flower drawn in fine hatching, layered pointed petals opening around a beaded seed-pod crown, with a small closed bud rising at the top. No caption or spell-script — a decorative/devotional lotus frontispiece marking a section rather than a tattoo stencil, the lotus as emblem of purity and blossoming merit.

pages.desc: A small decorative vignette centred on a blank leaf: a symmetrical lotus flower rising from a paired base, flanked left and right by two fish (or fish-tailed birds) with feathery plumed sprays curling
pages.descFull: A small decorative vignette centred on a blank leaf: a symmetrical lotus flower rising from a paired base, flanked left and right by two fish (or fish-tailed birds) with feathery plumed sprays curling upward. No spell-script or caption — an ornamental pair-and-lotus motif (a prosperity/auspicious emblem of the twin fish and the flower) used as a section decoration rather than a working yant.

pages.desc: A decorative sak-yant vignette of two facing birds, drawn mirror-image with scaled bodies, upright crests and long dripping plumed tails that curl toward each other. No caption or spell-script — a pai
pages.descFull: A decorative sak-yant vignette of two facing birds, drawn mirror-image with scaled bodies, upright crests and long dripping plumed tails that curl toward each other. No caption or spell-script — a paired-bird ornament of the metta-mahaniyom family (charm, coupling, sweet regard), a light figural motif for filling or bordering a tattoo rather than a formal yant.

pages.desc: A sampler leaf of six small charm motifs for tracing, ungrouped: top-left a Ganesha head crowned with an Om glyph (remover of obstacles, success); top-right a rearing naga serpent (water-lord, protect
pages.descFull: A sampler leaf of six small charm motifs for tracing, ungrouped: top-left a Ganesha head crowned with an Om glyph (remover of obstacles, success); top-right a rearing naga serpent (water-lord, protection, fortune); centre a pair of fish — one ornate with plumed fins, one plain koi (abundance, fertility); bottom a small tied money-bag and a bottle-gourd/namtao (wealth, containment). These are the stock small emblems a tattooist keeps to fill a composition, each a compact auspicious sign.

pages.desc: Two blank money-bag templates for tracing, one above the other: the upper a round pouch with a lotus-frilled neck, its face ruled into an empty quartered grid ready to take a four-syllable Na-square;
pages.descFull: Two blank money-bag templates for tracing, one above the other: the upper a round pouch with a lotus-frilled neck, its face ruled into an empty quartered grid ready to take a four-syllable Na-square; the lower a plain round bag tied with a bow, its face left empty. Unfilled outlines — the wealth-bag (thung ngoen thung thong) frame a practitioner completes by inking the seed-syllables into the quarters.

pages.desc: Two gourd motifs: above, a small bottle-gourd (namtao) tied at the neck; below, a larger gourd with a tokay gecko (tukkae) climbing it, splay-toed feet gripping the swell, tail curling into a spiral.
pages.descFull: Two gourd motifs: above, a small bottle-gourd (namtao) tied at the neck; below, a larger gourd with a tokay gecko (tukkae) climbing it, splay-toed feet gripping the swell, tail curling into a spiral. The gourd is a vessel of holy water and containment of fortune; the tukkae is a mahaniyom charm of allure and calling-in of wealth. Together a prosperity-and-attraction stencil.

pages.desc: Gecko charm stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) with star-splayed toes flanking a central lotus blossom, tails curling outward; below, a single gecko poised over a looped heart-shape. The tukka
pages.descFull: Gecko charm stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) with star-splayed toes flanking a central lotus blossom, tails curling outward; below, a single gecko poised over a looped heart-shape. The tukkae is a metta-mahaniyom emblem — its call summons luck and admirers — and paired around the lotus it doubles the draw. Light figural motifs for attraction and popularity.

pages.desc: More gecko stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) crossing tails in a symmetrical mirror, star-toed feet spread; below, a single gecko reaching toward a small round fruit or gourd it holds in its
pages.descFull: More gecko stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) crossing tails in a symmetrical mirror, star-toed feet spread; below, a single gecko reaching toward a small round fruit or gourd it holds in its forefeet. Spare, elegant line drawings of the tukkae charm-animal — a mahaniyom sign of allure, calling-in and good luck, kept as small tattoo motifs.

pages.desc: Two lotus stencils in fine-line contemporary style, one above the other: the upper a pointed-petal lotus half-opened around a sharp central bud, the lower a rounder full-bloom lotus with a heart-shape
pages.descFull: Two lotus stencils in fine-line contemporary style, one above the other: the upper a pointed-petal lotus half-opened around a sharp central bud, the lower a rounder full-bloom lotus with a heart-shaped seed-pod. No spell-script or caption — clean decorative lotus flash of the modern sak-yant/tattoo repertoire, the lotus standing for purity, merit and unfolding fortune.

pages.desc: Two lotus motifs, finely hatched: above, an ornate frontal lotus rosette opened flat like a mandala around a beaded seed-crown; below, a closed lotus bud on a long curling stem that resolves into a sm
pages.descFull: Two lotus motifs, finely hatched: above, an ornate frontal lotus rosette opened flat like a mandala around a beaded seed-crown; below, a closed lotus bud on a long curling stem that resolves into a small looped unalom-and-heart flourish at its tip. Decorative contemporary flash without spell-script — the lotus as emblem of purity and blossoming, the trailing unalom lending a devotional finish.

pages.desc: A symmetrical fine-line composition: a central lotus blossom flanked left and right by two ornate birds with long feathered sweeping plumes curling upward and back. No caption or script — a decorative
pages.descFull: A symmetrical fine-line composition: a central lotus blossom flanked left and right by two ornate birds with long feathered sweeping plumes curling upward and back. No caption or script — a decorative lower-back / sternum style flash pairing the lotus (purity, fortune) with mirrored birds (metta, coupling, sweet regard), in the modern tattoo idiom.

pages.desc: A fine-line stencil: a full lotus blossom above, with two peacocks below facing inward, their long feathered tail-sprays fanning up and outward on either side. No script or caption — a decorative peac
pages.descFull: A fine-line stencil: a full lotus blossom above, with two peacocks below facing inward, their long feathered tail-sprays fanning up and outward on either side. No script or caption — a decorative peacock-and-lotus flash, the peacock a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty and allure paired with the lotus of purity and blossoming fortune.

pages.desc: A fine-line stencil: a pointed-petal lotus at centre with two swallows/birds in flight below it, wings drawn as sweeping feathered plumes curling outward. No caption or spell-script — a decorative bir
pages.descFull: A fine-line stencil: a pointed-petal lotus at centre with two swallows/birds in flight below it, wings drawn as sweeping feathered plumes curling outward. No caption or spell-script — a decorative bird-and-lotus flash in the modern idiom, the paired birds carrying the metta sense of charm and coupling beneath the lotus.

pages.desc: A fine-line stencil: two peacocks facing inward flank a small round money-bag tied at the neck, their long ornate tail-feathers sweeping down and out in mirrored arcs. No caption or script — a decorat
pages.descFull: A fine-line stencil: two peacocks facing inward flank a small round money-bag tied at the neck, their long ornate tail-feathers sweeping down and out in mirrored arcs. No caption or script — a decorative peacock-and-money-bag flash that fuses the peacock (beauty, allure, mahaniyom) with the wealth-bag (prosperity), a charm for both charm and fortune.

pages.desc: Two fine-line bird stencils: above, two long-tailed birds facing outward with scrolled plumed tails sweeping apart in mirror; below, two birds flanking a small bottle-gourd (namtao), their tails cross
pages.descFull: Two fine-line bird stencils: above, two long-tailed birds facing outward with scrolled plumed tails sweeping apart in mirror; below, two birds flanking a small bottle-gourd (namtao), their tails crossing and braiding beneath it. No caption or spell-script — decorative paired-bird flash of the metta-mahaniyom kind, the lower design tying the birds (charm, coupling) to the gourd (containment of fortune).

pages.desc: Two fine-line stencils: above, two parrots/lovebirds set head-to-head so their bodies and folded wings form a heart (a love/coupling motif); below, a plump owl perched on a round orb or fruit, feather
pages.descFull: Two fine-line stencils: above, two parrots/lovebirds set head-to-head so their bodies and folded wings form a heart (a love/coupling motif); below, a plump owl perched on a round orb or fruit, feathers finely scaled. No caption or script — decorative flash, the paired birds a charm for love and the owl a sign of watchful wisdom and, in the mahaniyom sense, drawing-in of fortune.

pages.desc: A single fine-line peacock standing with its tail fanned upright behind it in a spray of eyed plumes, body finely scaled, small crest on the head. No caption or spell-script — a decorative peacock ste
pages.descFull: A single fine-line peacock standing with its tail fanned upright behind it in a spray of eyed plumes, body finely scaled, small crest on the head. No caption or spell-script — a decorative peacock stencil, the peacock a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty, radiance and allure, drawn as standalone flash for shoulder or back.

pages.desc: Two fine-line stencils of Thai mythical serpent-birds (naga- or makara-headed hong in the kranok idiom), each drawn in flight with a crested serpentine head, scaled body and flame-scrolled tail. No ca
pages.descFull: Two fine-line stencils of Thai mythical serpent-birds (naga- or makara-headed hong in the kranok idiom), each drawn in flight with a crested serpentine head, scaled body and flame-scrolled tail. No caption or script — decorative Lanna-ornament flash of the water/protection creatures, elegant filler figures rather than script-bearing yant.

pages.desc: A single fine-line phoenix/hong rising with wings spread wide and a long tail resolving into a cascade of ink-scroll spirals below. Small crest and open beak. No caption or spell-script — decorative f
pages.descFull: A single fine-line phoenix/hong rising with wings spread wide and a long tail resolving into a cascade of ink-scroll spirals below. Small crest and open beak. No caption or spell-script — decorative flash of the celestial bird (grace, auspicious ascent, high status), drawn as a flowing standalone piece for back or thigh.

pages.desc: An ornate fine-line firebird/phoenix in profile, wings and body finely scaled, with a great plumed peacock-eyed tail sweeping up and back in flame-like fronds. No caption or script — decorative flash
pages.descFull: An ornate fine-line firebird/phoenix in profile, wings and body finely scaled, with a great plumed peacock-eyed tail sweeping up and back in flame-like fronds. No caption or script — decorative flash of the auspicious bird, a metta-and-beauty emblem drawn as a flowing standalone tattoo rather than a script-bearing yant.

pages.desc: Two fine-line rooster stencils, one above the other: a proud cockerel (kai) with arched sickle tail-feathers and finely scaled breast, and below a second cock striding with raised hackles. No caption
pages.descFull: Two fine-line rooster stencils, one above the other: a proud cockerel (kai) with arched sickle tail-feathers and finely scaled breast, and below a second cock striding with raised hackles. No caption or spell-script — decorative fighting-cock flash, the kai an emblem of courage, valour and fighting spirit (kong / boldness), drawn as standalone motifs.

pages.desc: Two fine-line butterfly stencils: above, a symmetrical pair of open butterfly wings (drawn as a mirrored pair, split at the body); below, a single butterfly banked to one side with trailing scrolled l
pages.descFull: Two fine-line butterfly stencils: above, a symmetrical pair of open butterfly wings (drawn as a mirrored pair, split at the body); below, a single butterfly banked to one side with trailing scrolled lower wings. No caption or script — decorative butterfly flash of the metta-mahaniyom kind, a charm of beauty and attraction meant to make the wearer alluring.

pages.desc: Fish stencils in fine line: above, two round-bodied fish (gourami/pla) facing nose-to-nose with ruffled fins; below, a single koi-style carp swimming upward, body finely scaled. No caption or spell-sc
pages.descFull: Fish stencils in fine line: above, two round-bodied fish (gourami/pla) facing nose-to-nose with ruffled fins; below, a single koi-style carp swimming upward, body finely scaled. No caption or spell-script — decorative fish flash, the fish an auspicious emblem of abundance, fertility and flowing fortune, kept as filler or standalone motifs.

pages.desc: A single fine-line leaping deer (kwang) in mid-bound, its antlers stylised into curling flame-fronds, a patterned kranok band looping around its neck and haunch. No caption or spell-script — decorativ
pages.descFull: A single fine-line leaping deer (kwang) in mid-bound, its antlers stylised into curling flame-fronds, a patterned kranok band looping around its neck and haunch. No caption or spell-script — decorative deer flash; the kwang carries a metta-mahaniyom sense of gentleness and grace and recalls the deer of the Buddha first sermon, drawn as a flowing standalone piece.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a four-armed deity astride Garuda: Phra Narai (Vishnu) crowned with a tiered mongkut, bearing discus, conch, club and trident in his hands, mounted on the winged bird-man Khrut wh
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a four-armed deity astride Garuda: Phra Narai (Vishnu) crowned with a tiered mongkut, bearing discus, conch, club and trident in his hands, mounted on the winged bird-man Khrut whose wings spread wide and talons grip below. No script or caption — a divine mount motif (Narai song Khrut) carrying power, kingship and protection, drawn as a bold standalone figure for the back.

pages.desc: Two figural stencils of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior in acrobatic leaping poses, one above the other, mirrored diagonally. Each wears a crown and patterned loincloth, body finely tattooed with orn
pages.descFull: Two figural stencils of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior in acrobatic leaping poses, one above the other, mirrored diagonally. Each wears a crown and patterned loincloth, body finely tattooed with ornament, tail curling, one fist brandishing a trident/phra-khan. No caption or script — Ramakien warrior flash carrying kong-krapan (invulnerability), strength and boldness, drawn as bold standalone figures.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a yaksha (giant) in the Ramakien idiom: a crowned, fanged demon-warrior striding with a long sword raised overhead in one hand and a bow held in the other, ornamented armour and s
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a yaksha (giant) in the Ramakien idiom: a crowned, fanged demon-warrior striding with a long sword raised overhead in one hand and a bow held in the other, ornamented armour and sash, sinuous scroll-clouds below his feet. No caption or script — a guardian-giant motif carrying fierce protective power and the driving-off of harm, a bold back-piece figure.

pages.desc: An intricate circular figural stencil: a fanged guardian face at the centre wrapped in a churning knot of naga serpents, banners and blades radiating outward, all densely ornamented. No caption or scr
pages.descFull: An intricate circular figural stencil: a fanged guardian face at the centre wrapped in a churning knot of naga serpents, banners and blades radiating outward, all densely ornamented. No caption or script — a knotted guardian/demon composition (a fierce protective emblem in the mural idiom), the coiling nagas and weapons packing power and menace into a roundel for the back or shoulder.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of Hanuman leaping, the crowned white monkey brandishing a trident in one raised hand and a pennoned banner-staff in the other, tail curling, body finely ornamented. No caption or sc
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of Hanuman leaping, the crowned white monkey brandishing a trident in one raised hand and a pennoned banner-staff in the other, tail curling, body finely ornamented. No caption or script — Ramakien warrior flash carrying kong-krapan invulnerability and martial boldness, drawn as a dynamic standalone figure.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a crowned deva/warrior astride a mythical lion-beast (rachasi/singha), holding aloft a long pennoned banner-flag, the mount rearing with flame-scrolled mane and tail. No caption o
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a crowned deva/warrior astride a mythical lion-beast (rachasi/singha), holding aloft a long pennoned banner-flag, the mount rearing with flame-scrolled mane and tail. No caption or script — a deity-on-mount motif of rank, victory and protective power, drawn as a bold standalone figure.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaping, a sword raised in one hand, the other flung up, tail sweeping into an ornamented scroll beneath him, body crowned and finely patterned. N
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaping, a sword raised in one hand, the other flung up, tail sweeping into an ornamented scroll beneath him, body crowned and finely patterned. No caption or script — Ramakien warrior flash carrying kong-krapan invulnerability, agility and boldness, a dynamic standalone back or arm figure.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a crowned yaksha/deva warrior standing frontally on a woven knot of naga serpents, fanged face, ornamented armour and sash, a trident-blade in one hand. No caption or script — a g
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a crowned yaksha/deva warrior standing frontally on a woven knot of naga serpents, fanged face, ornamented armour and sash, a trident-blade in one hand. No caption or script — a guardian-giant figure treading the nagas, an emblem of dominating protective power and command over the serpent-world, a bold standalone back-piece.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a crouching figure drawing a great bow that arcs into a heart-shaped loop around him; his whole body — chest, thighs, arms — is covered in spiral koh-marks like an all-over protec
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a crouching figure drawing a great bow that arcs into a heart-shaped loop around him; his whole body — chest, thighs, arms — is covered in spiral koh-marks like an all-over protective tattoo, and his face is beast-like. No caption or script — an archer/hunter guardian figure sheathed in spiral yant, an emblem of readiness, protection and covered-in-power (metaa/kong), drawn as a striking standalone piece.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a striding tiger (suea) roaring, body fully striped, one forepaw raised with claws spread, tail lashing. No caption or script — the tiger is a paramount Lanna power-emblem carryin
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a striding tiger (suea) roaring, body fully striped, one forepaw raised with claws spread, tail lashing. No caption or script — the tiger is a paramount Lanna power-emblem carrying kong-krapan invulnerability, fierce authority (amnat) and command; a bold standalone back or chest figure, one of the great sak-yant beasts.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a roaring tiger head (na suea), jaws open with bared fangs and lolling tongue, whiskers and ruff drawn as flaming fronds, striped brow. No caption or script — the tiger-face is a
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a roaring tiger head (na suea), jaws open with bared fangs and lolling tongue, whiskers and ruff drawn as flaming fronds, striped brow. No caption or script — the tiger-face is a fierce power-and-authority emblem (kong-krapan, amnat, commanding presence), drawn as a compact standalone motif for shoulder or nape.

pages.desc: Two prowling-beast stencils: above, a striped tiger (suea) creeping head-down with tail trailing; below, a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) in the mural idiom, flame-scrolled mane and clawed feet.
pages.descFull: Two prowling-beast stencils: above, a striped tiger (suea) creeping head-down with tail trailing; below, a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) in the mural idiom, flame-scrolled mane and clawed feet. No caption or script — both are power-and-protection emblems (the tiger for kong-krapan and authority, the singha for guardian strength), drawn as standalone figures.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) prowling, its dragon-like crested head thrown up, flame-scroll mane, ridged spine and clawed feet, tail curling into a flame-frond. No capti
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) prowling, its dragon-like crested head thrown up, flame-scroll mane, ridged spine and clawed feet, tail curling into a flame-frond. No caption or script — a guardian-lion emblem of strength, ferocity and protective power in the Lanna mural idiom, drawn as a bold standalone figure.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a Lanna guardian-lion (singha/rachasi) prowling in the mural idiom: crested dragon-like head with bared teeth and cross-hatched maw, flame-frond mane and ridged joints, clawed fee
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a Lanna guardian-lion (singha/rachasi) prowling in the mural idiom: crested dragon-like head with bared teeth and cross-hatched maw, flame-frond mane and ridged joints, clawed feet, tail curling into flame. No caption or script — a temple-guardian emblem of ferocity and protective power, drawn as a bold standalone figure for the back.

pages.desc: Two serpent stencils: above, a single snake rising in an S-curve, its lower body looped into a heart; below, two snakes rearing head-to-head from an interlaced love-knot of their own braided bodies. N
pages.descFull: Two serpent stencils: above, a single snake rising in an S-curve, its lower body looped into a heart; below, two snakes rearing head-to-head from an interlaced love-knot of their own braided bodies. No caption or script — naga/snake motifs binding protection with the heart-and-knot sense of union and holding-fast, decorative charm figures of water-power and bound affection.

pages.desc: A figural stencil of a ruesi (lersi) in profile: a bearded ascetic-sage with hooded brow and long goatee, crowned with a tall flame-tipped headdress and wearing a strand of prayer-beads across the bar
pages.descFull: A figural stencil of a ruesi (lersi) in profile: a bearded ascetic-sage with hooded brow and long goatee, crowned with a tall flame-tipped headdress and wearing a strand of prayer-beads across the bare shoulder. No caption or script — this is the hermit-teacher (a Pho Kae / ruesi likeness) honoured as master and protector in the wai khru, placed on the skin as the source-figure of the craft.

pages.desc: A sheet of two teacher/deity heads: above, a frontal crowned face with a tiered pointed mongkut and calm features (a thewada/deva head); below, a profile bearded ruesi (lersi) in a flame-tipped crown
pages.descFull: A sheet of two teacher/deity heads: above, a frontal crowned face with a tiered pointed mongkut and calm features (a thewada/deva head); below, a profile bearded ruesi (lersi) in a flame-tipped crown with a long wispy goatee. No caption or script — devotional master-and-deity heads of the wai-khru repertoire, placed on the skin as protectors and lineage-sources of the sak-yant craft.
